items.title: Read 'Bias in the Loop' (LLM-as-judge for SWE)
items.desc: 20-min skim of arXiv 2604.16790 — see paper card below. Direct read on MAE's 10 LLM-judge evaluators: prompt-bias sensitivity flips relative model rankings. Note any control they propose that you could borrow as a calibration probe.

sections.title: 🎯 Today's single side-project focus (D009)
sections.text: **morning-brief** — drain the review queue end-to-end, then freeze. **Crumbs, BedRock, Gestgo on hold** until a side-project decision flips tomorrow. MAE (day job) runs in parallel and is unaffected.

sections.title: 📄 Paper of the day — LLM-judge prompt bias breaks model rankings

paper.title: Bias in the Loop: Auditing LLM-as-a-Judge for Software Engineering
paper.authors: Zixiao Zhao, Amirreza Esmaeili, Fatemeh Fard
paper.tldr: Pointwise LLM-judges across code-gen, code-repair and test-gen flip their verdicts under semantics-preserving prompt edits. Effects are large enough to swap which model 'wins' a benchmark.
paper.why: Maps 1:1 onto MAE's eval suite — if the 10 judge evaluators absorb prompt artifacts, the 'eval-driven dev' loop is optimizing against noise. The authors' bias-sensitivity score is a cheap calibration probe to add alongside accuracy.
key_findings: Repeated runs of the same judge on the same code disagree non-trivially — judges are not deterministic graders.
Single-cue prompt interventions (ordering, framing, hints) systematically shift preferences toward the cued option.
When the cue aligns with the gold answer, accuracy rises; when it doesn't, accuracy falls sharply — net validity collapses.
Bias effects can flip task-level conclusions and reorder model rankings. Reported gains may be prompt artifacts.
Recommendation: report bias sensitivity alongside accuracy; add explicit controls in any LLM-judge study.

sections.title: 📰 Industry pulse — 3 from HN front page

items.title: DeepSeek v4
items.summary: DeepSeek v4 lands. Top of HN by a wide margin, and the open-weight pressure on closed labs continues to compound.
items.why: Affects MAE eval choices — if v4 is competitive on code/agent tasks, it's an obvious cheap judge to triangulate against your existing 10-evaluator panel.

items.title: Google plans to invest up to $40B in Anthropic
items.summary: Google adds up to $40B to its Anthropic position. Cements Claude as a structurally-funded incumbent through the next cycle.
items.why: Your day-to-day rides on Claude (Hermes, Code) — supply-side stability matters. Also a market signal for where agentic-AI vendor spend is going.

items.title: Sabotaging projects by overthinking, scope creep, and structural diffing
items.summary: Practitioner essay on the failure mode of refactoring and pre-planning instead of shipping. Concrete examples + counter-patterns.
items.why: Direct read on D009 (one side project per day). The 'context-switch tax across Crumbs / BedRock / morning-brief' you already named is exactly the trap he describes.
